patterns_recognized: HARD CAP 1 (negative-edge) must be treated as a non-negotiable hard SKIP regardless of tier — BAL@LAD PLATINUM lost 12-1 on 6/21 despite a pre-cap A- stack and Claude CONFIRM; do not chase negative-edge PLATINUM/GOLD/DIAMOND picks today (OAK@DET, COL@LAD, ARI@SD all have CAP1 live).

HARD CAP 10 bottom-bucket (pick_prob in 0.50-0.55) CONFIRM-rescues only pay off with a real anchor — SP xERA edge >=1.0 agreeing with the pick, or a genuine OWNER BvP cluster. The pre-cap-A rescue exception with no anchor failed twice (BOS@SEA 6/20 and 6/21). Today only BOS@CHW (SP edge 1.28, F3=7787.7) clears that bar; CHC@BAL, ATL@PIT, ARI@SD do not.

PLATINUM tier keeps running ~43% historically, and when grade_reasons explicitly flag 'SP edge unconfirmed' as the PLATINUM driver (as with ATL@PIT today), that's a red flag, not a badge — treat as SKIP-caliber until a real SP or BvP signal shows up.

F1* (thin Statcast sample) is only trustworthy when F3_swing_take_gap > 1000 corroborates it (validated 6/13 SEA@WSH). OAK@DET today has F1*=1.40 with F3=1664.9, which clears the bar, but HARD CAP 1's negative edge still overrides regardless of that corroboration.

Always read the CSV pick column verbatim before reasoning — Claude Brain misread it on at least 3 occasions in June (TOR@CHC, CHW@DET, PIT@COL) by inferring direction from F-signal or away-SP xERA instead. Every pick below was cross-checked against the CSV pick column directly.

TOR @ SF.reasoning: Clean D-grade fade: Webb (SF home SP) carries a 1.10 xERA disadvantage vs Cease, and PQI (-25.6) opposes SF as well, with Stage 1/2 only mildly agreeing. Two independent structural signals both point away from the pick, matching the reliable 'dual-opposing-signal' D-grade pattern validated repeatedly in June (e.g. 6/18 TOR@BOS, BAL@SEA, CHW@NYY). No qualitative reason to fight it.

CHC @ BAL.reasoning: Bottom-bucket pick_prob=0.507 with only PQI (+24.9) and Stage agreement backing it — no SP edge, no F-signal, and confidence_downgrade=True. This is exactly the thin-anchor profile that HARD CAP 10 rescues have failed on repeatedly (BOS@SEA 6/20-6/21 both lost without an SP-edge>=1.0 or OWNER-BvP anchor). No anchor here either, so I'm not rescuing the tier — SKIP stands.

OAK @ DET.reasoning: Genuinely decent stack — F1*=1.40 corroborated by F3=1664.9 (clears the F1*-needs-F3>1000 bar) and SP xERA agrees by 1.54 for Melton over Springs. But HARD CAP 1 fired at -3.92pp edge, and negative-edge GOLD has proven non-negotiable all season (COL@OAK 6/14 -17.48pp cost a 14-run blowout; BAL@LAD 6/21 -4.27pp lost 12-1). Market pricing overrides the signal stack here.
lessons_applied: F1*+F3 corroboration satisfied but insufficient vs CAP1

ATL @ PIT.reasoning: Pre-cap score is a strong 6 (A), but grade_reasons explicitly say 'SP edge unconfirmed' — the PLATINUM label has no SP or OWNER-BvP anchor behind it, just F3=771 (below the 1000-2000 high-conviction floor) and PQI. PLATINUM's ~43% historical hit rate plus two recent failed CAP10 pre-cap-A rescues without an anchor (BOS@SEA 6/20, 6/21) argue against rescuing this one. bullpen_fatigue + confidence_downgrade=True add further caution.

NYY @ TB.reasoning: D-grade with SP xERA 0.94 AGAINST the pick (Cole should out-pitch McClanahan) and PQI -12.7 against TB, Stage 1/2 barely agree. Straightforward dual-opposing-signal fade, no qualitative reason to override given Cole's known reliability edge over McClanahan.

HOU @ WSH.reasoning: D-grade with SP xERA 0.68 against the pick (Griffin/WSH modestly favored over Arrighetti) and a near-coin-flip pick_prob=0.504. No F-signal, no PQI confirmation cited. Nothing here overrides the base read; standard SKIP confirmation.

CLE @ MIN.reasoning: The best-supported directional read on the slate outside of BOS@CHW: SP xERA agrees by 0.41 for Prielipp, Stage 1/2 agree closely (delta=0.05), only a modest PQI late-game-degradation concern (-3.3) pulls it off an A. No hard caps fired. Tier is SKIP per the pipeline's own banding, and I have no qualitative basis to push past that, but directionally this is a clean, low-risk CONFIRM.
lessons_applied: clean B-grade CONFIRM, no red flags

BOS @ CHW.reasoning: Strongest pre-cap stack on the slate (score=10, A): F3=7787.7 is well above the 2000 high-conviction floor, SP xERA agrees by a full 1.28 (Martin over Bennett... reversed, actually favors BOS), PQI confirms (-22.7), Stage 1/2 agree exactly. This clears the SP-edge>=1.0 anchor bar that recent CAP10 rescues have needed, so I'm confirming with elevated tier — but keeping it at GOLD rather than PLATINUM since even anchored CAP10 rescues only hit ~47.6% historically, below breakeven.

LAA @ TEX.reasoning: D-grade with SP xERA 0.59 against the pick and PQI -13.2 against TEX, plus an acute_roster stress flag and confidence_downgrade=True. This is the classic acute_roster + negative-signal combination that has reliably predicted losses across the June archive; no reason to elevate.

COL @ LAD.reasoning: The signal stack is genuinely strong (F2=0.037, F3=13716.5, F5_bullpen_gap=+1.75, PQI +7.3), but HARD CAP 1 fired at -1.89pp and confidence_downgrade=True is also live. This is the same double-flag combination that sank BAL@LAD 12-1 on 6/21 despite an even stronger pre-cap A- PLATINUM profile — negative-edge caps have proven non-negotiable regardless of how good the qualitative stack looks. Not chasing it.

ARI @ SD.reasoning: Single-signal stack (F5_bullpen_gap=+1.14 only, no SP edge cited) sitting in the bottom-bucket pick_prob=0.513 band with confidence_downgrade=True and HARD CAP 1 (-5.96pp) both live, plus acute_roster stress. No anchor to justify a CAP10 rescue, and CAP1's negative edge is decisive on its own per this season's pattern.

SEA @ MIA.reasoning: Both probable pitchers (George Kirby for SEA, Tyler Phillips for MIA) are now announced per live statsapi as of this run, but the quantitative pipeline had not yet scored this game when the diag CSV was generated — no F-signals, PQI, or SP-xERA gap available to evaluate. No directional call is possible from this file; defer to the next pipeline run.

KC @ NYM.reasoning: Probable pitchers (Steven Cruz for KC, Christian Scott for NYM) are now live per statsapi, but the diag CSV was generated before this game was scored — no quantitative signals available. No directional call possible from this file.

PHI @ CIN.reasoning: PHI's probable SP is still unannounced per live statsapi (only Cincinnati's Chase Burns is confirmed). Hard stop is correctly held; no directional call possible.

MIL @ STL.reasoning: Probable pitchers (Kyle Harrison for MIL, Michael McGreevy for STL) are now live per statsapi, but the diag CSV was generated before this game was scored — no quantitative signals available. No directional call possible from this file.
